note: Verifier agent-A: TYPE-MATCH VERIFIED. lem-intertwine is abstract (elements F,Phi of unital Banach algebras A,B; intertwiner j between the BASE spaces those elements act on; products = element-left-mult composed with j; convergence uses element-submultiplicativity ||a.x||<=||a|| ||x||, explicitly NOT endomorphism-composition). Correct instantiation: abstract-A=End(D) acting on base D_sa, abstract-B=End(B(H)_sa) acting on base B(H)_sa, abstract-intertwiner = parent's j:B(H)_sa->D_sa. Checks: (i) Fj=jPhi via F=jC,Phi=Cj => Fj=jCj=jPhi (maps B(H)_sa->D_sa) OK; (ii) F is <1/4-idempotent as element of End(D): ||F^2-F||_op<=||.||_cb<=eta<1/4 OK; Phi<1/4 by N1.1 OK; (iii) j bounded (||j||_op=1) unital -- and lem-intertwine's own note records unitality is NOT load-bearing. MINOR: node wording 'F acting on D, i.e. on the unital Banach algebra End-side' is loose -- F is the ELEMENT of End(D), and the intertwiner is between BASE spaces not End-spaces; lem-intertwine H3's phrase 'underlying Banach spaces of the two algebras' is itself imprecise but the operative requirements actually used match the parent exactly. Sound.
